title: Dockerfile with squash: true complains that docker daemon needs experimental flags enabled
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Dockerfile with the squash: true parameter:
```
devprod-ci-android-sdk-gradle-agent:
  squash: true
  build_directory: .
  build: |
    USER root
...
```

fails with 
```
FATAL ERROR: Docker error message:
   400 Client Error for https://127.0.0.1:43211/v1.41/build?t=1.devprod-ci-android-sdk-gradle-agent.dmk%3Aa4880cf3-7877-4303-aa7a-25136d191bcd&q=False&nocache=False&rm=True&forcerm=False&pull=False&squash=True: Bad Request ("squash is only supported with experimental mode")

Using `squash` and/or `secret_files` requires a docker daemon with experimental features enabled. See
    https://github.com/docker/docker-ce/blob/master/components/cli/experimental/README.md
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. create Dockerfile with squash parameter set to true

2. try building the docker file (the header to this bug report mentions using buildah but the link is invalid)

3. see error

**Describe the results you received:**
fails with 
```
FATAL ERROR: Docker error message:
   400 Client Error for https://127.0.0.1:43211/v1.41/build?t=1.devprod-ci-android-sdk-gradle-agent.dmk%3Aa4880cf3-7877-4303-aa7a-25136d191bcd&q=False&nocache=False&rm=True&forcerm=False&pull=False&squash=True: Bad Request ("squash is only supported with experimental mode")

Using `squash` and/or `secret_files` requires a docker daemon with experimental features enabled. See
    https://github.com/docker/docker-ce/blob/master/components/cli/experimental/README.md
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
build of dockerfile is successful

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
run via minikube --driver=podman
and then docker build

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
OSX M1 macbook...minikube...did "brew upgrade" to make sure I had the latest.
Also tried adding "experimental":"true" to ~/.docker/config.json and restarting everything w/o luck.

comments.body: That doesn't look like a Podman error message. Are you sure Podman is in use, and not Docker?

"docker version" didn't show any running server until podman and minikube were started.
I did look through the podman and buildah repos for that error msg and couldn't find it so I'm a bit baffled as well.

I also didn't see anything about setting experimental for the docker engine inside podman.  Is there an offiical way of setting it or does Podman just not support "squash: true" in the Dockerfile? (I noticed there's a --squash param though).

If you run minikube with `--driver=podman`, it will run the default runtime (docker or containerd) in a podman container.

If you want to use "podman" for the kubernetes cluster, then you also need to add `--container-runtime=cri-o`

But it does not actively change your docker configuration, unless you also run something like `minikube docker-env` ?

In this particular issue, I think that "docker info" would give more insight than the output from "podman info"...

> But it does not actively change your docker configuration, unless you also run something like `minikube docker-env` ?

I do use:
"eval $(minikube -p minikube docker-env)"
to set the docker env.
I thought podman also included it's own docker daemon..is it just a wrapper over docker?  
 
> In this particular issue, I think that "docker info" would give more insight than the output from "podman info"...

Looks like regular docker:

docker info
Client:
 Context:    default
 Debug Mode: false
 Plugins:
  buildx: Docker Buildx (Docker Inc., v0.9.1)
  compose: Docker Compose (Docker Inc., v2.10.2)
  extension: Manages Docker extensions (Docker Inc., v0.2.9)
  sbom: View the packaged-based Software Bill Of Materials (SBOM) for an image (Anchore Inc., 0.6.0)
  scan: Docker Scan (Docker Inc., v0.19.0)

@kenyee Podman is not a wrapper over docker, its entirely different engine/container manager and the error you are seeing is actually coming from `docker` not `podman`. So your environment is using `docker` and not `podman`.

> Also tried adding "experimental":"true" to ~/.docker/config.json and restarting everything w/o luck.

Those are the **client** settings.

To change the server settings, you need to do `minikube ssh` and then edit /etc/docker/daemon.json

And restart the daemon to take effect.

You can also the edit the systemd unit files as usual, see the docker (or the systemd) documentation.

> Podman is not a wrapper over docker, its entirely different engine/container manager and the error you are seeing is actually coming from `docker` not `podman`. So your environment is using `docker` and not `podman`.

Actually the environment is using **both** (nested containerization). But since it is not possible to use podman-in-podman (it does not support k8s), you will have to use crio-in-podman if you want to replace the default docker-in-docker configuration...

`minikube start --driver=podman --container-runtime=cri-o`

(the configuration reported uses docker-in-podman)

Currently `docker-env` is not supported for cri-o runtime, only `podman-env`

In a future release, the legacy docker api will be available for all of them

You can also use `minikube image`

Most likely, Podman endpoint will be deprecated (in minikube, that is). Too much struggle with the clients and incompatible API.

> To change the server settings, you need to do `minikube ssh` and then edit /etc/docker/daemon.json

Ahh...that was a bit I was missing.

Would love to see all this info above in the podman docs 😄 
thx

> Would love to see all this info above in the podman docs 😄

I don't think it has anything to do with Podman, but it would be good to update the minikube docs if you find them missing info 

https://minikube.sigs.k8s.io/docs/drivers/podman/

https://minikube.sigs.k8s.io/docs/runtimes/cri-o/

> I don't think it has anything to do with Podman

For the Podman part, the explanation above about how it interacts w/ docker is what I found useful and didn't see it in the docs.
It is a bit complex, you are running docker pods in a podman system container running in CoreOS on a macOS system... 4 different layers

Currently minikube is missing documentation about both Podman Desktop on Mac (or Linux) but also Docker Desktop on Linux.

Since both of them already include their own Kubernetes, it is unclear on where the docs should reside ? We made a longer presentation about it, on KubeCon (the environments)

Updating the set of steps to set docker to experimental mode in case anyone else finds this:

- podman machine set --cpus=4 --disk=160
- podman start
- minikube start --driver=podman
- minikube ssh
- sudo vi /etc/docker/daemon.json
- put this into the file:
{ "experimental" : "true" }
- exit ssh
- minikube stop
- minikube start --driver=podman
- docker version -f '.Server.Experimental'
- this should return "true"

This is for the osx/docker/podman/minikube combo.
